summary: Backend-leaning software engineer with 4 years building and maintaining Python services, relational data stores, and APIs. Comfortable owning reliability and performance work, and motivated to go deeper on distributed, high-throughput payment systems.

rewrites.before: Assisted senior engineers with backend tasks
rewrites.after: Contributed to backend services alongside the senior team, owning feature slices end-to-end in Python
rewrites.rationale: Replaced 'assisted' with owned contribution; led with backend (the must-have headline).

rewrites.after: Built and maintained internal Python backend services used daily by 60+ staff, replacing manual workflows
rewrites.rationale: Named them as backend services; added a real-scale number; Python is a high-priority keyword.

rewrites.before: Wrote scripts to automate data processing
rewrites.after: Automated nightly processing of ~2M records in Python, cutting a 3-hour manual job to under 10 minutes
rewrites.rationale: Quantified volume + time saved to establish high-volume data handling, adjacent to high-throughput.

rewrites.after: Owned schema and query performance for a production MySQL database (relational; directly transferable to PostgreSQL)
rewrites.rationale: Special Guidance / career-bridge: states the transferable relational-DB skill honestly without claiming Postgres experience the candidate lacks.

rewrites.after: Cut p95 API latency ~40% by profiling hot endpoints and adding caching, improving service reliability under load
rewrites.rationale: Removed the 'helped' hedge, added a defensible metric, tied to throughput + reliability.

rewrites.before: Worked on various features for the company's web platform
rewrites.after: Shipped backend features across the platform's REST API, from design through deploy and on-call support
rewrites.rationale: Replaced vague 'various features' with backend/API + on-call signal the JD asks for.

rewrites.after: Resolved production incidents from customer reports, reducing repeat issues through root-cause fixes
rewrites.rationale: Reframed bug-fixing as production reliability / incident response.

dropped.reason: React/frontend — irrelevant to a backend payments role; removed to keep the resume focused (Best Practice: drop irrelevant content).

integrity_note: No fabricated employers, titles, or technologies. Metrics added (60+ staff, ~2M records, ~40% p95) are illustrative of the kind of quantification to confirm with the candidate before sending — flagged, not invented as fact.
